content_body:
If you're looking for a unique, memorable, and family-friendly experience to add to your North Georgia itinerary, search no further than the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway! This historic train ride is one of the essential attractions of Blue Ridge and has served hundreds of thousands of passengers over the years. Taking its passengers on a journey through the picturesque Blue Ridge Mountains, the BR Scenic Railway offers stunning views of the Toccoa River, a chance to unwind on the GA/TN state line, all while preserving its rich history. In this article, we'll explore the saga of this heritage railroad, its special events, and our luxury cabins that overlook the train tracks!

The History of the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway

Without the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway, Blue Ridge, GA might not be the tourist destination it is today. Originally, The Blue Ridge Scenic Railway was built in 1886 as part of the Marietta and North Georgia Line. Assisting with the mining and timber industry, the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway shipped mail and other products in the early 1900s. In 1905, the Blue Ridge Depot opened, foreshadowing its legacy over 100 years later. In 1950, the last passenger train departed followed by M&NG's close 37 years later.

However, in 1998, the railway was revived as a tourist attraction, offering visitors a chance to experience the beauty of the Blue Ridge Mountains from a unique perspective. Allowing passengers to ride the original 26-mile round trip from Blue Ridge to McCaysville, GA/Copperhill, TN, this crucial attraction serves about 80,000 passengers per year. Today, the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway is a popular destination for families, train enthusiasts, and anyone looking for a nostalgic journey through time!

The Journey

Once departing from the historic depot in downtown Blue Ridge, the train travels along the Toccoa River, offering breathtaking and peaceful views of the water and national forest. The 26-mile round trip takes approximately four hours, with a one-hour layover in the twin cities of McCaysville, GA/Copperhill, TN. During the layover, you can explore this small town's charming shops and restaurants, take photos at the state line, and stop by the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway gift shop to pick up a souvenir from the journey!

Mountain Scenery

One of the highlights of the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway is the stunning mountain scenery. As the train winds its way through the valleys of the North Georgia mountains, passengers are treated to panoramic views of the Toccoa River. Take a step back from the hustle and bustle and soak in the views from this calm train ride. Keep your camera handy to capture the breathtaking vistas along the way!

Seasonal Events

In addition to the regular train rides, the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway also offers special events throughout the year. From their "Santa Express" during the holiday season (complete with hot chocolate, Santa Claus, and more!), a "Firecracker Special" on the 4th of July, and "Fall Foliage" rides in October, these events are extremely popular with families and often sell out, so be sure to book your tickets in advance.

Toccoa River Luxury Cabins

For those looking to enjoy the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway both on and off the ride, our luxury Toccoa River cabins offer stunning waterfront and mountain views, top-of-the-line amenities, and easy access to downtown Blue Ridge. Strategically located along the train tracks, these North Georgia cabin rentals allow guests to enjoy the sights and sounds of this beloved Blue Ridge mountain train from the comfort of their second home.

Riverview Lodge

As the name suggests, Riverview Lodge offers stunning views of the Toccoa River and mountain ranges from its elevated position on the ridge line. Accommodating 11 guests, this luxury lodge is the perfect choice for those who want to immerse themselves in the beauty of the Blue Ridge Mountains while enjoying the comfort of a gorgeous cabin. Boasting 5 bedrooms/5.5 bathrooms, Riverview Lodge also sports a pool table, hot tub, and plenty of outdoor seating.

Fallen Timber Lodge

Situated closer to the picturesque Toccoa River, Fallen Timber Lodge provides guests with a cozy cabin experience combined with a front-row seat to the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway. Located just below Outlaw Ridge and Riverview Lodge, imagine sipping your morning coffee on the porch while absorbing the sights and sounds of the Toccoa River, creating unforgettable memories. Sleeping up to 12 guests, this 5 bedroom/5.5 bathroom lodge boasts 2 twin day beds in addition to 5 King/Queen beds as well as a pool table, arcade tables, a hot tub, and an octagonal deck for al fresco dining.

The Rivers Edge

Located 0.2 miles down the road from the previous three cabins, The Rivers Edge provides a tranquil setting for guests to relax and unwind. Watch from any of the three spacious porches or the octagonal deck perched just above the river as the train makes its way through the scenic landscape, adding an extra touch of charm to your stay. This 5 bedroom, 3.5 bathroom cabin rental boasts similar amenities to Fallen Timber Lodge!

Plan Your Trip

If you're planning a trip to visit Blue Ridge, GA and ride the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway, be sure to check the schedule and book in advance. The train operates from March to December, with limited rides in January and February. Tickets can be purchased online or at the depot, but it's recommended to book in advance to secure your preferred date and time.

Experience the beauty and nostalgia of the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway for yourself. All aboard!
title: All Aboard! The Blue Ridge Scenic Railway
